    17‚Üí- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/132](https://www.erdosproblems.com/132)`.
    18‚Üí- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (limited-occurrence distances in planar point sets).
    19‚Üí- [x] Bibliography entries: `[Er84c]`, `[ErPa90]`, `[HoPa34]`, `[ErFi95]`, `[CDL25]` with full citation details.
    20‚Üí
    21‚Üí## 4. Namespace
    22‚Üí
    23‚Üí- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos132 ... end Erdos132`.
    24‚Üí- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos132`.
    25‚Üí- [x] `open Classical` appears **before** the namespace block.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí## 5. Custom Definitions
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí- [x] `pairCount` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    30‚Üí- [x] `IsLimitedOccurrence` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    31‚Üí- [x] `limitedOccurrences` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    32‚Üí- [x] `pairCount` is marked `noncomputable` (involves `Finset.filter` with decidability from `open Classical`).
    33‚Üí- [x] `limitedOccurrences` is marked `noncomputable` (depends on `pairCount`).
    34‚Üí- [x] `IsLimitedOccurrence` is a `def` returning `Prop` ‚ÄĒ does not need `noncomputable`.
    35‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
    36‚Üí- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsLimitedOccurrence`.
    37‚Üí- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `pairCount`, `limitedOccurrences`.
    38‚Üí- N/A: No local notation needed.
    39‚Üí- N/A: No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí## 6. Theorem Statements
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí- [x] Both use the `theorem` keyword.
    44‚Üí- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_132` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    45‚Üí- [x] Variant is named `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity`.
    46‚Üí- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
    47‚Üí- [x] Both have `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 52]`.
    48‚Üí- [x] Both theorems have docstrings (`/-- ... -/`).
    49‚Üí- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: `[Er84c, ErPa90, ErFi95]` and `[Er84c, ErPa90]`.
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí### `answer()` usage
    52‚Üí
    53‚ÜíThe module docstring states: *"Must there be two distances which occur at least once but between at most n pairs of points? Must the number of such distances tend to infinity as n ‚Üí ‚ąě?"* ‚ÄĒ both are yes/no questions (**Case 1**).
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí- [ ] **FAIL**: `erdos_132` should use `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` form, but currently states `P` directly (Case 3 style). The problem asks "Must there be‚Ä¶?" which is a yes/no question. Should be:
    56‚Üí  ```lean
    57‚Üí  theorem erdos_132 :
    58‚Üí      answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    59‚Üí        ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), 5 ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
    60‚Üí          2 ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
    61‚Üí    sorry
    62‚Üí  ```
    63‚Üí- [ ] **FAIL**: `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity` has the same issue. Should be:
    64‚Üí  ```lean
    65‚Üí  theorem erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity :
    66‚Üí      answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    67‚Üí        ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), N ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
    68‚Üí          k ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
    69‚Üí    sorry
    70‚Üí  ```
    71‚Üí- [x] Quantifiers are not theorem parameters (they are universally quantified in the body, which is correct).
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí## 7. Category Attribute
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí- [x] Both theorems have **exactly one** `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
    76‚Üí- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
    77‚Üí- [x] Both research open theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí## 8. AMS Attribute
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí- [x] Both theorems have **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 52`.
    82‚Üí- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 52).
    83‚Üí- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 52.
    84‚Üí- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    85‚Üí- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 52 (convex and discrete geometry) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí## 9. Proof Bodies
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí- [x] Both open conjectures use `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
    90‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    91‚Üí- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
    92‚Üí- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. The docstring provides mathematical context (partial results by Hopf‚ÄďPannwitz, ErdŇĎs‚ÄďFishburn, Clemen‚ÄďDumitrescu‚ÄďLiu), not proof sketches.
    93‚Üí- [x] No `native_decide`.
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí- [x] Variant is named `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity` (correct naming convention).
    98‚Üí- [x] Variant is in the **same file** as the main theorem.
    99‚Üí- [x] Variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes (same as main: `research open, AMS 5 52`).
   100‚Üí- [x] Variant has a docstring documenting its relation to the main problem (part 2: number of limited-occurrence distances tends to infinity).
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí## 11. Code Quality
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
   105‚Üí- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č†`, `‚Č§`, `‚Üí`.
   106‚Üí- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
   107‚Üí- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
   108‚Üí- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
   109‚Üí- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí## 12. Build Verification
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üí- [ ] `lake build` ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.

   116‚Üí## Summary

   119‚Üí
   120‚ÜíBoth `erdos_132` and `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity` are formalized as direct assertions (Case 3) but the original problem asks yes/no questions ‚ÄĒ *"Must there be‚Ä¶?"* and *"Must the number ‚Ä¶ tend to infinity?"* ‚ÄĒ so they should use `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` form (Case 1). Everything else is clean: copyright header, imports, module docstring with thorough bibliography, namespace structure, custom definitions with appropriate docstrings and `noncomputable` markings, correct naming conventions, valid category and AMS attributes, and good code quality.

# Review: deepmind/132.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 132`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/132](https://www.erdosproblems.com/132)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (limited-occurrence distances in planar point sets).
- [x] Bibliography entries: `[Er84c]`, `[ErPa90]`, `[HoPa34]`, `[ErFi95]`, `[CDL25]` with full citation details.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos132 ... end Erdos132`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos132`.
- [x] `open Classical` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `pairCount` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `IsLimitedOccurrence` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `limitedOccurrences` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `pairCount` is marked `noncomputable` (involves `Finset.filter` with decidability from `open Classical`).
- [x] `limitedOccurrences` is marked `noncomputable` (depends on `pairCount`).
- [x] `IsLimitedOccurrence` is a `def` returning `Prop` ‚ÄĒ does not need `noncomputable`.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsLimitedOccurrence`.
- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `pairCount`, `limitedOccurrences`.
- N/A: No local notation needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Both use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_132` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Variant is named `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity`.
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Both have `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 52]`.
- [x] Both theorems have docstrings (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: `[Er84c, ErPa90, ErFi95]` and `[Er84c, ErPa90]`.

### `answer()` usage

The module docstring states: *"Must there be two distances which occur at least once but between at most n pairs of points? Must the number of such distances tend to infinity as n ‚Üí ‚ąě?"* ‚ÄĒ both are yes/no questions (**Case 1**).

- [ ] **FAIL**: `erdos_132` should use `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` form, but currently states `P` directly (Case 3 style). The problem asks "Must there be‚Ä¶?" which is a yes/no question. Should be:
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_132 :
      answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
        ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), 5 ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
          2 ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
    sorry
  ```
- [ ] **FAIL**: `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity` has the same issue. Should be:
  ```lean
  theorem erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity :
      answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
        ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), N ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
          k ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
    sorry
  ```
- [x] Quantifiers are not theorem parameters (they are universally quantified in the body, which is correct).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Both theorems have **exactly one** `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
- [x] Both research open theorems contain `sorry` in their proof bodies.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Both theorems have **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 52`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 52).
- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 52.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 52 (convex and discrete geometry) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Both open conjectures use `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. The docstring provides mathematical context (partial results by Hopf‚ÄďPannwitz, ErdŇĎs‚ÄďFishburn, Clemen‚ÄďDumitrescu‚ÄďLiu), not proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variant is named `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity` (correct naming convention).
- [x] Variant is in the **same file** as the main theorem.
- [x] Variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes (same as main: `research open, AMS 5 52`).
- [x] Variant has a docstring documenting its relation to the main problem (part 2: number of limited-occurrence distances tends to infinity).

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č†`, `‚Č§`, `‚Üí`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters ‚ÄĒ not verified in this review.

## Summary

**Result: FAIL** ‚ÄĒ 2 issues found

Both `erdos_132` and `erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity` are formalized as direct assertions (Case 3) but the original problem asks yes/no questions ‚ÄĒ *"Must there be‚Ä¶?"* and *"Must the number ‚Ä¶ tend to infinity?"* ‚ÄĒ so they should use `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` form (Case 1). Everything else is clean: copyright header, imports, module docstring with thorough bibliography, namespace structure, custom definitions with appropriate docstrings and `noncomputable` markings, correct naming conventions, valid category and AMS attributes, and good code quality.

content.text: The review failed due to both theorems needing `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P` form. Let me read the current file.

1‚Üí/-
     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3‚Üí
     4‚ÜíLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5‚Üíyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6‚ÜíYou may obtain a copy of the License at
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9‚Üí
    10‚ÜíUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 132
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/132](https://www.erdosproblems.com/132)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $A \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of $n$ points. Must there be two
    25‚Üídistances which occur at least once but between at most $n$ pairs of points?
    26‚ÜíMust the number of such distances tend to infinity as $n \to \infty$?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíAsked by ErdŇĎs and Pach. A "limited-occurrence distance" for $A$ is a value
    29‚Üí$d > 0$ such that the number of ordered pairs $(x, y)$ with $x \neq y$ in $A$
    30‚Üíand $\mathrm{dist}(x, y) = d$ is between $1$ and $n = |A|$ inclusive.
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíHopf and Pannowitz [HoPa34] proved that the largest distance between points
    33‚Üíof $A$ can occur at most $n$ times, making it a limited-occurrence distance
    34‚Üíwhenever it is realized. The question is whether a *second* such distance
    35‚Üímust also always exist.
    36‚Üí
    37‚ÜíErdŇĎs believed that for $n \geq 5$ there must always exist at least two
    38‚Üílimited-occurrence distances. ErdŇĎs and Fishburn [ErFi95] proved this for
    39‚Üí$n = 5$ and $n = 6$. Clemen, Dumitrescu, and Liu [CDL25] proved it for
    40‚Üípoint sets in convex position.
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[Er84c] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some old and new problems in combinatorial geometry*, 1984.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí[ErPa90] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pach, J., *Variations on the theme of repeated distances*, 1990.
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí[HoPa34] Hopf, H. and Pannwitz, E., *Aufgabe Nr. 167*, 1934.
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí[ErFi95] ErdŇĎs, P. and Fishburn, P., *Maximum planar sets that determine $k$ distances*, 1995.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí[CDL25] Clemen, F., Dumitrescu, A., and Liu, R., *Limited-occurrence distances in convex
    51‚Üíposition*, 2025.
    52‚Üí-/
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üíopen Classical
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üínamespace Erdos132
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- For a finite point set $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$ and a real value $d$,
    59‚Üíthe number of ordered pairs $(x, y)$ with $x \neq y$ in $A$ at
    60‚ÜíEuclidean distance $d$. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def pairCount (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) (d : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚Ąē :=
    62‚Üí  ((A √óňĘ A).filter (fun p => p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß dist p.1 p.2 = d)).card
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/-- A distance $d$ is a *limited-occurrence distance* for $A$ if it is
    65‚Üíachieved by at least one but at most $|A|$ ordered pairs of distinct
    66‚Üípoints of $A$. -/
    67‚Üídef IsLimitedOccurrence (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) (d : ‚ĄĚ) : Prop :=
    68‚Üí  0 < pairCount A d ‚ąß pairCount A d ‚Č§ A.card
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/-- The set of all limited-occurrence distances for $A$. -/
    71‚Üínoncomputable def limitedOccurrences (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) : Set ‚ĄĚ :=
    72‚Üí  {d : ‚ĄĚ | IsLimitedOccurrence A d}
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí/--
    75‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 132, Part 1 [Er84c, ErPa90, ErFi95]:
    76‚ÜíFor any set $A$ of $n \geq 5$ points in the plane $\mathbb{R}^2$, there
    77‚Üímust exist at least two distinct limited-occurrence distances.
    78‚Üí-/
    79‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 52]
    80‚Üítheorem erdos_132 :
    81‚Üí    ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), 5 ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
    82‚Üí      2 ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/--
    86‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 132, Part 2 [Er84c, ErPa90]:
    87‚ÜíThe number of limited-occurrence distances must tend to infinity with $n$.
    88‚ÜíFor every $k$, there exists $N$ such that any set $A$ of at least $N$ points
    89‚Üíin $\mathbb{R}^2$ has at least $k$ limited-occurrence distances.
    90‚Üí-/
    91‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 52]
    92‚Üítheorem erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity :
    93‚Üí    ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), N ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
    94‚Üí      k ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
    95‚Üí  sorry
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üíend Erdos132
    98‚Üí

/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 132

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/132](https://www.erdosproblems.com/132)

Let $A \subset \mathbb{R}^2$ be a set of $n$ points. Must there be two
distances which occur at least once but between at most $n$ pairs of points?
Must the number of such distances tend to infinity as $n \to \infty$?

Asked by ErdŇĎs and Pach. A "limited-occurrence distance" for $A$ is a value
$d > 0$ such that the number of ordered pairs $(x, y)$ with $x \neq y$ in $A$
and $\mathrm{dist}(x, y) = d$ is between $1$ and $n = |A|$ inclusive.

Hopf and Pannowitz [HoPa34] proved that the largest distance between points
of $A$ can occur at most $n$ times, making it a limited-occurrence distance
whenever it is realized. The question is whether a *second* such distance
must also always exist.

ErdŇĎs believed that for $n \geq 5$ there must always exist at least two
limited-occurrence distances. ErdŇĎs and Fishburn [ErFi95] proved this for
$n = 5$ and $n = 6$. Clemen, Dumitrescu, and Liu [CDL25] proved it for
point sets in convex position.

[Er84c] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some old and new problems in combinatorial geometry*, 1984.

[ErPa90] ErdŇĎs, P. and Pach, J., *Variations on the theme of repeated distances*, 1990.

[HoPa34] Hopf, H. and Pannwitz, E., *Aufgabe Nr. 167*, 1934.

[ErFi95] ErdŇĎs, P. and Fishburn, P., *Maximum planar sets that determine $k$ distances*, 1995.

[CDL25] Clemen, F., Dumitrescu, A., and Liu, R., *Limited-occurrence distances in convex
position*, 2025.
-/

open Classical

namespace Erdos132

/-- For a finite point set $A \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$ and a real value $d$,
the number of ordered pairs $(x, y)$ with $x \neq y$ in $A$ at
Euclidean distance $d$. -/
noncomputable def pairCount (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) (d : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚Ąē :=
  ((A √óňĘ A).filter (fun p => p.1 ‚Č† p.2 ‚ąß dist p.1 p.2 = d)).card

/-- A distance $d$ is a *limited-occurrence distance* for $A$ if it is
achieved by at least one but at most $|A|$ ordered pairs of distinct
points of $A$. -/
def IsLimitedOccurrence (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) (d : ‚ĄĚ) : Prop :=
  0 < pairCount A d ‚ąß pairCount A d ‚Č§ A.card

/-- The set of all limited-occurrence distances for $A$. -/
noncomputable def limitedOccurrences (A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) : Set ‚ĄĚ :=
  {d : ‚ĄĚ | IsLimitedOccurrence A d}

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 132, Part 1 [Er84c, ErPa90, ErFi95]:
For any set $A$ of $n \geq 5$ points in the plane $\mathbb{R}^2$, there
must exist at least two distinct limited-occurrence distances.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 52]
theorem erdos_132 :
    ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), 5 ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
      2 ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
  sorry

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 132, Part 2 [Er84c, ErPa90]:
The number of limited-occurrence distances must tend to infinity with $n$.
For every $k$, there exists $N$ such that any set $A$ of at least $N$ points
in $\mathbb{R}^2$ has at least $k$ limited-occurrence distances.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5 52]
theorem erdos_132.variants.tend_to_infinity :
    ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ A : Finset (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2)), N ‚Č§ A.card ‚Üí
      k ‚Č§ Set.ncard (limitedOccurrences A) := by
  sorry

end Erdos132
